subject: Product Management
title: Scope Creep
description: A quick win: understand Scope Creep.

hook.text: 'While we're at it, let's also add...' — the seven most dangerous words in product development.

buildup.text: Scope creep is the gradual expansion of a project beyond its original boundaries. Each addition seems small and reasonable.

discovery.text: A two-week project becomes a two-month project through 'small' additions. Each adds a day. Twenty additions add twenty days.

twist.text: Scope creep isn't caused by bad people — it's caused by optimism. Everyone underestimates the compound cost of additions.

climax.text: The cure: define a clear scope document before starting. Any addition requires removing something else of equal effort.

punchline.text: Add something? Remove something. That's the only way scope stays controlled.

quiz.question: How should teams prevent scope creep?
options: Require that adding anything means removing something of equal effort
Allow all additions since each one is small
Never define scope in advance to stay flexible
